message:
fix(test): pipe stdout/stderr in integration test run_am when stdin is piped

The run_am helper in integration_runs.rs used Stdio::piped() for stdin
but left stdout/stderr inherited from the parent process. This caused
child process output to appear in the test runner's terminal instead of
being captured in the Output struct, making assertions on out.stderr
fail silently. Fixes 2 guard_check integration tests. (br-2ei.5.7.2.1)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-2ei.5.7","title":"CLI Tests: unit + integration + golden help + JSON stability","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive CLI test suite covering parsing, help output, JSON mode, integration behavior, and exit codes.

## Scope
- Unit tests for clap parsing/defaults (br-2ei.5.7.1).
- Integration tests for real command execution in temp dirs (br-2ei.5.7.2).
- Golden help output snapshots (br-2ei.5.7.3).
- JSON output stability tests (br-2ei.5.7.4).

## Tests
- `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli` covering all above categories.
- E2E CLI harness (br-2ei.9.5) for high‑signal smoke.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Help snapshots under `tests/fixtures/cli_help/`.
- JSON fixtures under `tests/fixtures/cli_json/`.
- Failure artifacts under `tests/artifacts/cli/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All behavior and requirements described above are implemented with legacy parity.
2. Unit/integration tests cover the described cases with deterministic outputs and logged artifacts.
3. Any referenced E2E coverage passes and captures the expected logs/artifacts.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"GreenDune","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:50:04.178085704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:08:57.497197740Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.5.7","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:50:04.178085704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.5.7.1","title":"CLI Tests: unit parsing + defaults","description":"Unit tests for CLI parsing and defaults.

Coverage:
- clap arg parsing for serve, share, guard, doctor, mail/acks/reservations commands.
- products commands, archive commands, docs insert-blurbs, migrate, clear-and-reset-everything, amctl env, am-run.
- default values (ports, paths, output format) match legacy spec (br-2ei.5.6).
- env var overrides (where legacy supported).
- exit code semantics for invalid args.

Logging:
- When failures occur, print full parsed-args diff and environment snapshot.

Dependencies:
- br-2ei.5.1, br-2ei.5.2, br-2ei.5.3, br-2ei.5.4, br-2ei.5.5, br-2ei.5.6

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Unit tests cover parsing for all major subcommands and validate defaults against the legacy spec.
2. Env var overrides are tested with explicit fixtures for supported variables.
3. Invalid arg combinations return the expected exit code and error message.
4. Failures print a parsed-args diff and environment snapshot to aid debugging.
5. Tests are deterministic and do not require network or external  {"id":"br-2ei.5.7.2","title":"CLI Tests: integration runs + exit codes","description":"Integration tests that run the CLI binary end-to-end in temp dirs.

Coverage:
- serve-stdio and serve-http startup/shutdown (dry-run where possible).
- guard install/status/uninstall in a temp git repo.
- share export/update/verify/preview/decrypt + wizard (script stub) with seeded project data.
- doctor check/repair stubs (no destructive actions).
- products commands, archive save/list/restore, docs insert-blurbs, migrate, clear-and-reset-everything, amctl env/am-run.

Logging/artifacts:
- Store stdout/stderr for each command under tests/artifacts/cli/integration/<timestamp>/.
- Print expected vs actual exit codes and key output lines.

Dependencies:
- br-2ei.5.1–5.5, br-2ei.5.6

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Integration tests execute the CLI binary in isolated temp dirs and clean up all processes they start.
2. Commands listed in coverage are exercised and assert expected exit codes plus required output markers.
3. All stdout/stderr is captured and written under tests/artifacts/cli/integration/<timestamp>/ with per-case headers.
4. Tests do not use destructive git/fs commands; guard install/uninstall uses a temp repo only.
5. Failures print clear diagnostics (command line, exit code, key output  {"id":"br-2ei.5.7.3","title":"CLI Tests: golden help snapshots","description":"Golden help output snapshots for CLI parity.

Coverage:
- Top-level help output.
- Key subcommands: serve, share, guard, doctor, products, archive, docs insert-blurbs, migrate, clear-and-reset-everything, amctl env, am-run.
- Ensure help text, flag ordering, defaults, and examples match legacy spec (br-2ei.5.6).

Logging/artifacts:
- Store golden outputs under tests/fixtures/cli_help/.
- On mismatch, print unified diffs and save actual outputs under tests/artifacts/cli/help/<timestamp>/.

Dependencies:
- br-2ei.5.1–5.6

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Golden help snapshots exist for top-level and listed subcommands and are checked in under `tests/fixtures/cli_help/`.
2. Tests compare help output exactly (including flag ordering and defaults), with normalization limited to documented nondeterminism.
3. Non-TTY execution produces colorless output and is part of the snapshot set.
4. On mismatch, a unified diff is printed and actual outputs are stored under `tests/artifacts/cli/help/<timestamp>/`.
5. Tests are deterministic and run in CI without needing network or external  {"id":"br-2ei.5.7.4","title":"CLI Tests: JSON output stability","description":"JSON output stability tests for CLI commands that expose machine-readable data.

Coverage:
- Ensure JSON output schemas match legacy (field names, nesting, nullability).
- Verify stable ordering where legacy is stable (or document normalization).
- Commands: guard status/check, share verify/preview, doctor check, list-projects, list-acks, products search/inbox/status, archive list, acks pending/remind/overdue, file_reservations list/active/soon.

Logging/artifacts:
- Store expected JSON fixtures under tests/fixtures/cli_json/.
- On mismatch, emit JSON diff and save actual outputs under tests/artifacts/cli/json/<timestamp>/.

Dependencies:
- br-2ei.5.1–5.6

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Tests run under `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli` (or workspace equivalent) and cover all listed JSON-producing commands.
2. JSON schemas match legacy fixtures (field names/types/nullability); any normalization rules are documented in the fixtures.
3. Ordering is stable where required (or normalized before comparison) and asserted in tests.
4. On mismatch, tests print a JSON diff and store actual outputs under `tests/artifacts/cli/json/<timestamp>/`.
5. Tests are deterministic and pass on a clean temp env (no reliance on machine-specific paths).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleHawk","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:51:01.253361843Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:17:44.975960142Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:17:44.975937740Z","close_reason":"Generated  {"id":"br-2ei.5.8","title":"CLI: products subcommands parity (ensure/link/status/search/inbox/summarize-thread)","description":"Port and verify the legacy product-bus CLI commands.

Scope (match legacy Typer behavior):
- commands: products ensure, products link, products status, products search, products inbox, products summarize-thread.
- flags/args: product_key? defaults, --name/-n, --limit/-l, --urgent-only/--all, --include-bodies/--no-bodies, --since-ts, --per-thread-limit/-n, --no-llm.
- outputs: human (frankentui table) + --json mode with stable schemas.
- exit codes: invalid args, missing product, no links, etc.

Implementation notes:
- Use existing product tools / DB queries (avoid new business logic).
- Ensure consistent sorting and deterministic timestamps where legacy normalizes.
- Surface errors with clear, legacy-matching messages.

Tests (must be comprehensive):
- Unit: clap parsing, default values, env overrides (if any), invalid arg combos.
- Integration: temp DB + temp storage_root, exercise each command with 0/1/n results.
- JSON stability: golden fixtures for each command’s JSON output.
- E2E: include in scripts/e2e_cli.sh (or a dedicated product E2E case) and save artifacts.

Logging/artifacts:
- Store stdout/stderr under tests/artifacts/cli/products/<timestamp>/ on failure.
- Print expected vs actual diffs for JSON payload mismatches.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All listed products commands exist and match legacy flags/defaults/exit codes.
2. Human output uses frankentui tables; non-TTY disables color.
3. JSON mode outputs are schema-stable and covered by fixtures.
4. Integration tests run in isolation (temp dirs) and are deterministic.
5. E2E path captures artifacts and fails fast on mismatch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticOtter","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:13:29.512773709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:20:58.455989543Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T02:20:58.455963574Z","close_reason":"Completed products CLI parity (positional inbox agent, legacy fallback behavior) + added deterministic integration tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.5.8","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:13:29.512773709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
